content: mongodb-memory-server vs real MongoDB gaps: (1) Some advanced features unavailable: transactions behavior may differ across versions, full-text search, certain aggregation stages. (2) Must run tests sequentially (--runInBand) or use per-test isolated instances to avoid inter-test state bleed. (3) Version mismatch: memory-server pins a specific mongod binary version that may differ from production — index behavior, query planner, and error messages can differ. (4) No persistent state between test suites by default; seeding must be done programmatically. (5) Memory-server downloads mongod binary on first run (~60MB), causing slow CI cold starts. (6) beforeAll/afterAll setup is critical — forgetting to call stop() leaks open handles in Jest.
